analysis.classification_reasoning: Stock gapped from $4.68 to $8.70 on 5/29 (+86%) on 1.24M shares (8x prior avg ~150k), then held the gap and extended to $10.84 intraweek before closing $9.47 — retaining 77% of peak gain and closing the week +93%. The 5/29 gap had a news flag and is consistent with an FDA/RP1 regulatory update — fundamental driver present.
analysis.prediction_rationale: Base case is consolidation in the $8.50–$10.50 zone over the next 2-3 weeks. The gap is held, but Friday's -4% rejection from $10.45 on declining volume signals exhaustion at the round-number resistance, and Replimune historically chops post-catalyst until the next FDA datapoint. Without a fresh headline, momentum traders rotate out and the float settles; with one, the move resolves directionally on a gap, not a grind.

Base case is $3.80–$5.20 consolidation over the next 2-3 weeks with declining volume, unless management uses the elevated price to announce an ATM/secondary — which would force a leg down to retest the $3.20 pre-spike base.
